blocks.content: They came through the chapel doors into the afternoon.

The light outside was the May light — generous, angled, the same light Ethan had been trying to paint for months and had never quite landed. It fell on them now and he thought: that's it. That's what it looks like. Not a color. A feeling.

The guests spilled out behind them. Nathan appeared with the immediate energy of a man who has been sitting still for too long and has a great deal of opinions about what happens next. Maya appeared a step behind him — close, the particular closeness of someone who has gotten used to a proximity and no longer steps back from it.
Lena found Cassandra and held her face in both hands for a moment. No words. Just looked at her.
Thomas Hailey came last. He stood in front of his daughter and her husband — husband, new word, true word — and he looked at them both for a long moment.
He nodded. He hugged her. He shook Ethan's hand and then pulled him in too, briefly, the hug of a man who does not hug easily and means every hug he gives.
Cassandra looked at Ethan over her father's shoulder.
They were married. The afternoon was theirs. The future — uncertain and enormous and precious — was theirs.
